name: Booker T. Jones
abstract: Booker Taliaferro Jones Jr. (born November 12, 1944) is an American musician, songwriter, record producer and arranger, best known as the frontman of the band Booker T.

concerts.label: The Music of Prince
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Ruthie Foster
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Booker T in the context of Ardent Studios history; the interviewee responds about the studio's history but does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Booker T.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruthie Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 28, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: And it was like Booker T and the MGs in Memphis — two white guys and two black guys. And they played on Otis Redding... It was Booker T and the MGs. Booker T Jones, black, on keyboards...
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: James "Sing" Miller
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses a dog named 'Booker T' or 'T.' in the context of feeding it, with no indication of a personal or professional relationship with a person named Booker T.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Burbank
about_quotes.evidence: And the Glass, you know, Booker T. It was Booker T's job. He just hired the men, you know. ... We played out there about three or four times.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbert Tillman
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer states: 'I know Booker T played with him for a while.' Interviewee responds: 'Yeah. But he was probably playing bass drums.'

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Dawson
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, yeah. We played with him. You see, Booker T used to play in a, uh, regularly entertaining band, you know? Yeah. Dance band.
